title: pavonia lasiopetala
intro: Pavonia lasiopetala, also known as the rose mallow or wild hibiscus, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Malvaceae family. It is a perennial shrub that is native to the Americas and is known for its beautiful, showy flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, have been made from the plant's leaves and flowers to help alleviate symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
